<question>
1. a is olive.
2. a is south.
3. If a is north and a is olive, then b is coral.
4. If b is coral, then b is north.
5. If a is east and a is olive, then b is juniper.
6. If b is juniper, then b is north.
7. If a is south and a is olive, then b is elm.
8. If b is elm, then b is north.
9. If a is west and a is olive, then b is cobalt.
10. If b is cobalt, then b is north.
Which state applies to b?
</question>

<formal>
<constants>
a = a
b = b
</constants>
<predicates>
Ax: x is cobalt
Bx: x is coral
Cx: x is elm
Dx: x is juniper
Ex: x is olive
Fx: x is north
Gx: x is south
Hx: x is east
Ix: x is west
</predicates>

Ea
Ga

Bb -> Fb
Ha & Ea -> Db
Db -> Fb
Ga & Ea -> Cb
Cb -> Fb
Ia & Ea -> Ab
Ab -> Fb
</premises>
<proof>
Ea ; R
Ga ; R
Cb ; ->E
Fb ; ->E
</proof>
<conclusion>
Fb
</conclusion>
</formal>
